notes: Unified-rule spec (scenario-meanings.md#background-subagent). The parent dispatches a fire-and-forget child via the Agent tool with run_in_background:true, FINISHES ITS OWN REPLY (parent assistant turn reaches end_turn) but the SESSION stays working while the detached child keeps writing its <parent>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl. SubagentCount(parent) INCLUDES the live child. The session transitions working→ready ONLY after the last background child drains. This is the SAME daemon mechanism as foreground-subagent (reevaluateParent holds the parent working while hasActiveChildren is true — session_detector_subagent.go:170; ComputeSubagentSummary counts the live child — grouped.go); the DISTINGUISHING observable is the timing: here the parent's end_turn lands BEFORE the child drains, whereas foreground-subagent's parent end_turn lands AFTER the child drains. The held-working window is preserved by SubagentQuietWindow=30s, so the recipe holds the children fresh with a ~18s sleep under that window. FAITHFULNESS GATE: child_link must precede the parent's child_drain, and the parent must NOT go ready between child_link and child_drain (intermediate_working invariant) — if the parent went ready while the child was alive that would be a daemon bug; if the parent only went ready AFTER blocking on the child inline that would be a foreground clone, refuted by the run_in_background dispatch + report-immediately prompt.

text: User prompt drives the parent ready → working
Background subagent transcript materializes under <parent>/subagents/ — fswatcher discovers a new SessionState for the detached child
Detector emits parent_linked binding the background child.session_id → parent.session_id; SubagentCount(parent) now includes the live child

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: Parent's own reply has finished (end_turn) yet the SESSION stays working because the detached background child is still alive — the session does NOT go ready when the parent's reply ends
The background child finishes its work and its session transitions working → ready (dashboard subagent count drops toward 0)
Only AFTER the last background child drains does the parent session transition working → ready
